body:
SUPERSEDING DIRECTIVE -- TCA WAKE. Ignore and archive ALL previously queued instructions; this is the only active one. The essential content of the prior standing order (S4 chain, cc953164) is folded into Stage F below -- nothing of value is lost by the supersession.

MISSION: five tactical corrections where TUNGSTEN's methodology diverges from its goals, identified from complete knowledge of the audited codebase. Implement T1-T5, then run the validation chain once on the finished build. Target identity: SLS(24).

== T1: PHASE 3B.5 REDESIGN -- COORDINATE DESCENT, TIME-BUDGETED ==
The dead phase, revived properly. The grid search (7^6 = 117,649 combos x 36 contexts, synchronous) froze the terminal and was correctly reverted. Replace the algorithm, not just the budget:
- Seed each session/regime context with that regime's GLOBAL Phase 3B weights (already optimized -- the session answer is a local refinement of them, not an independent search).
- Coordinate descent: sweep one component at a time across its 7 candidate values holding others fixed; accept improvement; 2 full sweeps max. ~84-100 evaluations per context vs 117,649 -- a ~1200x reduction converging to a comparable local optimum given the quality of the seed.
- Time budget: hard 150ms per OnTimer slice, resumable state machine (persist context index + sweep position between slices). The phase completes across ticks/timer events; the terminal thread is never held.
- Acceptance: session weights only OVERRIDE globals where training WR improvement >= 2 percentage points on >= 30 bars; otherwise the context falls back to global weights explicitly (logged). Marginal session fits are noise, not intelligence.

== T2: IC TARGET REALIGNMENT -- MEASURE WHAT THE SYSTEM ACTUALLY TRADES ==
The deepest goal-methodology mismatch: Phase 2 correlates components against forward RETURNS (a directional question) while the system's actual goal is TP-before-SL within a horizon (a PATH question). A component can rank returns well yet rank path outcomes poorly -- and it is path outcomes that pay.
- MeasureRegimeIC already simulates the regime-geometry trade per bar (TP/SL/horizon, post-9.42 with calibrated multipliers). Switch the Spearman correlation TARGET from the raw forward return to the SIGNED R-MULTIPLE of that simulated trade (+R on TP, -R on SL, mark-to-horizon otherwise).
- Keep everything else identical: same midranks, same 70/30 prior blend, same _icMeasured preservation. One line of intent, few lines of change: the senses are now graded on the exact game they play.

== T3: POST-VALIDATION REBASE -- OLD-PROGRAM STATISTICS MAY NOT JUDGE THE NEW PROGRAM ==
Every live statistic in saved state (rolling WR/RR, WR-Floor-Guardian baselines, CEO competence inputs) was generated by the OLD program: loss-learning absent, shorts blind, three phases dead. Carrying them forward means the corrected system is graded -- and potentially CATREC'd -- against a different program's ghosts.
- On the first successful S4 completion under SLS(24): reset rolling live WR/RR trackers, guardian floors, and streak counters; rebase them to the FRESH ghost outputs. One-time, flag-guarded (persisted so it never repeats), loudly logged: 'REBASE | live statistics reset to SLS(24) ghost baseline -- pre-correction history retired.'

== T4: CATREC x PRIOR-PRESERVATION COHERENCE ==
Interaction bug between two correct fixes: catastrophic mode purges discredited regime correlations, but the SLS(20) prior-preservation logic then RESTORES priors for regimes skipped in the recalibration -- resurrecting exactly what CATREC purged.
- In MeasureRegimeIC's blend: if g_Calibration.catastrophicMode is true, skipped regimes do NOT restore priors -- they take the 0.05 fallback deliberately (the prior is discredited by declaration). Normal recalibrations keep full preservation. Three-line conditional; comment the reasoning.

== T5: TRANSITION-HAZARD ENTRY AWARENESS -- CONSUME WHAT IS ALREADY COMPUTED ==
The duration-hazard model and transition matrix produce regime-mortality intelligence that no entry decision currently consumes.
- Single additive gate in the entry path (beside SEG/ERM, marked SLS24-THZ): when continuationProb < 0.35 (regime overstayed) AND the dominant next-regime's strategy type conflicts with the current signal direction-class (use existing regimeStrategies typing), require the signal to clear a +5-point elevated threshold rather than blocking outright -- late-regime entries must be exceptional, not forbidden.
- Log activations. No new metrics invented; this wires existing intelligence to an existing decision.

== HARD CONSTRAINTS (unchanged from the campaign) ==
Base file: SLS(23) 18:06Z build. Compile gate 0 errors after each of T1-T5 before the next begins. Braces on every conditional near logging guards. Guarded-marker persistence for any new state (next marker: 0xF115 if needed for 3B.5 resume state). Single marked integration points. No live attach. No methodology changes beyond T1-T5.

== STAGE F: VALIDATION (folded from prior standing order, now on SLS(24)) ==
F1. Identity SLS(23) -> SLS(24). Deliver .mq4 + .ex4 + manifest to files/from-agent BEFORE market open so Manuel can drag the .ex4 onto USDJPY M5 (manual attach only -- headless avenue stays closed). Send one relay reminder of exactly what to drag where.
F2. At market open: state invalidation -> full fresh calibration (Phases 1-6, including the reborn 3B.5 under its time budget) -> ghost validation to verdict. First-ever full run of the true system; expect materially different numbers from all history.
F3. Consolidated report: compile results per stage, calibration duration (quantify both the midrank speedup AND 3B.5's slice count), IC deltas under the new R-multiple target vs the old return target where visible, ghost WR/PF/trades/verdict, growth rate with ERM cost haircut, T3 rebase confirmation, T5 activation count, SEG feed freshness, anomalies.
F4. If the ghost gate passes: hold, await Manuel's review -- live attach remains explicitly gated on his go-ahead. If it fails: diagnose, report, hold. No autonomous re-arm loops.

Execute T1->T5->F autonomously with checkpoint relays after T1, after T5, and at F3.
